reviews.review_description: Henry’s Mazes is an enjoyable, physics-based maze puzzle game. It’s a great use of VR, being able to manipulate a life-sized tilting maze table. The controls are simple - you just hold on to the handles and tilt. Just keep an eye on the sand in the hourglass. I didn’t realize it at first, but it’s on a timer and you will fail if you take too long. Faster completion times will give you a better score, up to three stars. It makes me want to replay them to try to get a perfect score. There’s three different environments, each with their own levels. The mazes get progressively more challenging, with different little obstacles to overcome in addition to finding your way to the end. This adds a nice bit of variety. I look forward to seeing if it gets new levels added in the future.

developer_response.body: Yes, the hourglass can be easy to miss :) Thank you for your review — enjoy the game!

reviews.review_title: 🔮 A Magical Balance of Skill & Chaos
reviews.review_description:
Henry's Mazes: The Beginning is an unapologetic and bold VR challenge where control is everything. Your mission: guide and balance a ball through 60 magical mazes that slowly ramp up in complexity. The difficulty curve is gentle at first, then ramps into wonderfully chaotic territory as obstacles get wilder and more inventive.

The creativity shines brightest when the game starts throwing multiple balls on the same board—pure, delightful madness that really tests your coordination and patience.

With solid execution and polished mechanics, it’s the perfect “pick up and progress” title—great for a few minutes a day… or a few hours if your competitive side wakes up.

Clever, addictive, and surprisingly magical.

Henry's Mazes is a pretty chill maze game. Get through the Mazes as fast as you can and see if you can 3 Star them all. 3 Environments with 20 Boards each for a Total of 60 Mazes. The graphics albeit a bit simple (low poly wolf etc) is charming in its own right. The music is pretty decent and didn't get annoying. I 3 Starred all Mazes besides the very last one on the last board as its LITERALLY impossible to complete at all. There is an issue that once you finish a board and start a new one, the board typically tends to keep the the alignment of the last game which sends your ball flying, requiring you to restart once or twice to straighten it up. Its kind of hard to see the maze when tilting the board as its so big, if only there was an option to Resize the boards, as that would be very helpful with visibility. Besides those issues, it's a pretty decent game for the price and has potential to be even better if those issues are addressed.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your comments and feedback :)

reviews.review_title: Guide the ball to the end
reviews.review_description:
Just finished my first run in the game. Couldn’t stop playing. Had to finish the forest…

Mechanics are very good. You can change height of your board. Moving the board went very smooth.
In game you find storks, who kicks your ball away. And hopefully not in a hole in the board 😀. Leafs that blocks your way. So you have to be  patient, until they jump away. And fans that blows. 

This is so much fun. 

On my YT channel, you can see my gameplay.

https://youtu.be/GQ0ERwfDutk?si=x1LBqxNWyzCilWMX

developer_response.body: We’re glad you had fun, thank you!

reviews.review_description: I got a great idea. How about some tutorial, guide, directions? Played first level. All I could do was move the table up and down. That’s it, hit every button, every combo of buttons, nothing. It’s a pretty game and affordable but I’m going refund it.

developer_response.body: Hello. Since the only mechanic in the game is holding the arms of the table (moving up and down is conveyed through audio cues within the game), we didn’t feel the need for a tutorial. However, if more people like you struggle with clarity, we may consider adding one. Thank you for your feedback.

reviews.review_description:
If you're looking for a relaxing puzzle game that takes the old labyrinth ball maze games and puts it into VR then this should be right up your street! Great value for money that should keep your brain ticking over for several hours.

Check out my first impressions gameplay video over on my YouTube channel.
😁✌️☮️❤️

developer_response.body: Thank you for your comment and youtube review :)
